sections.name: Skaveling
Skaveling Intellect
sections.text: As a curious side effect of their creation, skavelings absorb many memories of the ghoul brains on which they were fed. While merely an oversized animal in life, in undeath these memories coalesce into a strange form of intelligence that affords skavelings the ability to speak and reason, all the better to serve their urdefhan masters. Skavelings remain loyal to urdefhans and never take actions in a fight that would harm their creators.

One must perform a hideous ritual to make a skaveling. Though sometimes called ghoul bats, they're specifically crafted undead rather than true ghouls. The bloodsucking urdefhans of the Darklands raise giant bats on a toxic fungus and the flesh of ghouls—specifically their brains. Upon reaching maturity, these giant bats are ritually slain via poison. While this rots away the flesh of most creatures, the specially prepared bats immediately rise from death as skavelings.   
  
Despite their tattered wings and sagging skin, skavelings are quite capable of flight, even when carrying a creature on their back. Urdefhans often use them as mounts. Their intelligence is more advanced than that of a typical giant bat, and in combat they behave more like allies than animals, capable of making tactical decisions.

traits.name: Undead
traits.text: Once living, these creatures were infused after death with void energy and soul-corrupting unholy magic. When reduced to 0 Hit Points, an undead creature is destroyed. Undead creatures are damaged by vitality energy and are healed by void energy, and don’t benefit from healing vitality effects.

traits.name: Unholy
traits.text: Effects with the unholy trait are tied to powerful magical forces of cruelty and sin. They often have stronger effects on holy creatures. Creatures with this trait are strongly devoted to unholy causes, and often have weakness to holy. If a creature with weakness to unholy uses an unholy item or effect, it takes damage from its weakness.

interaction_abilities.name: Echolocation
interaction_abilities.text: A skaveling can use their hearing as a precise sense at the listed range.

traits.name: Agile
traits.text: The multiple attack penalty you take with this weapon on the second attack on your turn is –4 instead of –5, and –8 instead of –10 on the third and subsequent attacks in the turn.

ability.name: Bone-Chilling Screech

traits.name: Auditory
traits.text: Auditory actions and effects rely on sound. An action with the auditory trait can be successfully performed only if the creature using the action can speak or otherwise produce the required sounds. A spell or effect with the auditory trait has its effect only if the target can hear it. This applies only to sound-based parts of the effect, as determined by the GM. This is different from a sonic effect, which still affects targets who can’t hear it (such as deaf targets) as long as the effect itself makes sound.

traits.name: Emotion
traits.text: This effect alters a creature’s emotions. Effects with this trait always have the mental trait as well. Creatures with special training or that have mechanical or artificial intelligence are immune to emotion effects.

traits.name: Fear
traits.text: Fear effects evoke the emotion of fear. Effects with this trait always have the mental and emotion traits as well.

traits.name: Mental
traits.text: A mental effect can alter the target’s mind. It has no effect on an object or a mindless creature.

traits.name: Occult
traits.text:
This magic comes from the occult tradition, calling upon bizarre and ephemeral mysteries. Anything with this trait is magical.  
  
A creature with this trait is primarily constituted of or has a strong connection to occult magic.

ability.critical_success: The creature is unaffected and is temporarily immune to Bone-Chilling Screech for 24 hours.
ability.critical_failure: The creature is frightened 2 and stunned 1 by fear.
ability.text: The skaveling unleashes a horrifying screech that chills the very bones of those close enough to feel it. The screech can be heard for miles, but each creature in a 20-foot emanation must also attempt a DC 22 Will save. The skaveling can't use Bone-Chilling Screech again for 1d4 rounds.

ability.name: Consume Flesh

traits.name: Manipulate
traits.text: You must physically manipulate an item or make gestures to use an action with this trait. Creatures without a suitable appendage can’t perform actions with this trait. Manipulate actions often trigger reactions.

ability.requirement: The skaveling is adjacent to the corpse of a creature that died within the last hour
ability.effect: The skaveling devours a chunk of the corpse and regains 1d6 Hit Points plus 1d6 for every 2 levels the skaveling has. They can regain Hit Points from any given corpse only once.

ability.name: Feast on Fear

traits.name: Occult
traits.text:
This magic comes from the occult tradition, calling upon bizarre and ephemeral mysteries. Anything with this trait is magical.  
  
A creature with this trait is primarily constituted of or has a strong connection to occult magic.

ability.trigger: The skaveling deals damage to a frightened creature with a fangs Strike
ability.effect: The skaveling draws power from the fear infusing a creature's flesh. The frightened creature must attempt a DC 22 Fortitude saving throw.
ability.success: The creature takes a –5-foot status penalty to its Speed, and the skaveling gains a +5-foot status bonus to their Speeds until the end of their next turn.
ability.failure: The creature takes a –10-foot status penalty to its Speed, and the skaveling gains a +10-foot status bonus to their Speeds until the end of their next turn.
ability.critical_failure: The creature is slowed 1, and the skaveling can immediately Fly, Step, or Stride as a free action; this movement doesn't trigger reactions.

ability.name: Swift Dart

traits.name: Move
traits.text: An action with this trait involves moving from one space to another.

ability.text: The skaveling Flies up to half their Speed. This movement doesn't trigger reactions.
